title: Implement missing Python steps and align CLI behavior with specs
description: Bring Python Behave step coverage to parity with Gherkin specs and align CLI behavior (flags, workflows, interop) to pass all scenarios.
creator: ryan.porter

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Baseline (Feb 19, 2026): Python Behave hook error fixed by adding default epic transition_labels. behave --dry-run completes (no hook error). Full run not executed yet because conda/py311 not available in shell; need correct py311 invocation to capture pass/fail counts and coverage baseline.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Linked to epic decisions (see tskl-wti). Next: get conda py311 behave running, then align Python CLI/steps with decisions (single Beads store, dep syntax, missing flags).

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Baseline run via /opt/anaconda3/bin/conda run -n py311 python -m behave (Feb 19, 2026): 47 features passed, 3 failed, 7 skipped; 493 scenarios passed, 4 failed, 31 skipped; 2534 steps passed, 4 failed, 133 skipped. Failing scenarios: configuration_standardization.feature:14 (Missing configuration file fails), configuration_validation.feature:48 (Duplicate status names rejected), configuration_validation.feature:54 (Workflow statuses must exist), workflow/status_transitions.feature:46 (Type-specific workflow overrides default).

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Python: Behave now passing 57 features / 528 scenarios (0 failed, 0 skipped) under conda py311. Recent fixes: config loader missing file error, duplicate status and workflow validation, status validation now emits 'invalid transition' for non-workflow statuses so epic overrides are enforced. Next: run coverage (coverage run -m behave && coverage xml) to refresh 100% report; update TEST_COVERAGE_REPORT.md accordingly.
